explanation:
**Nutrition and fitness** are based on physical principles (energy balance) and biological principles (macronutrients, micronutrients). This calculator uses formulas validated by the scientific community (ACSM, ISSN, NIH).

## How It's Calculated

Calories in beer, wine, fernet, and gin.

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside normal limits.

## Standard Caloric Distribution

| Macronutrient | % of Calories | kcal per gram |
|---|---|---|
| Protein | 15-35% | 4 kcal/g |
| Carbohydrates | 45-65% | 4 kcal/g |
| Fat | 20-35% | 9 kcal/g |
| Alcohol | — | 7 kcal/g |
| Fiber | Minimum 25-38g/day | — |

## When to See a Nutritionist

- If you have health conditions (diabetes, hypertension, kidney disease) requiring a personalized diet.
- If you want to gain significant muscle mass or compete in sports.
- If you have disordered eating patterns (binge eating, severe restriction).
- If you haven't made progress toward your goal in more than 3 months.
- To plan complete vegan or vegetarian nutrition.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guideline only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How many calories are in a standard beer?
faq.a: A 12 oz (355ml) regular beer contains approximately 140-150 calories. Light beers average 90-100 calories, while craft beers range from 160-250+ calories depending on alcohol content and style.

faq.q: How many calories in a glass of wine?
faq.a: A 5 oz (150ml) glass of wine contains roughly 120-130 calories. Red wine ~125 kcal, white wine ~120 kcal, and rosé ~120 kcal.

faq.q: Does alcohol count toward your daily calorie intake?
faq.a: Yes. Alcohol contains 7 calories per gram—nearly as much as fat (9 kcal/g). Always count drink calories in your daily total for accurate weight loss and fitness tracking.

faq.q: Can you lose weight while drinking alcohol?
faq.a: Yes, but it's harder. Alcohol adds empty calories without nutrition and slows metabolism. Success requires tracking drink calories and maintaining a deficit elsewhere. Limit to 1-2 drinks per occasion.

faq.q: What's the difference between regular and light beer calories?
faq.a: Light beer has 90-100 calories per 12 oz versus 140-150 for regular. Light beers use fewer carbs and slightly lower alcohol content (3.5-4% ABV vs 4-5% ABV).

faq.q: How many calories in spirits like gin, vodka, and rum?
faq.a: A 1.5 oz (44ml) shot of 80-proof spirit contains approximately 95-110 calories. Calories increase with proof level. Mixers often add 20-150+ calories depending on type.

faq.q: Why does alcohol have so many calories?
faq.a: Alcohol contains 7 calories per gram—nearly double carbohydrates or protein (4 kcal/g) and almost as much as fat (9 kcal/g). Your body also metabolizes it inefficiently.

faq.q: What are the lowest-calorie alcoholic drinks?
faq.a: Light beer (~90-100 kcal), dry wine (~120 kcal), spirits with zero-calorie mixers (~95-110 kcal), and hard seltzers (~100 kcal). Avoid sugary mixers, sweet wines, and dessert drinks.
